payload.source_text: The pocket money tracker system allows children to claim pocket money tasks as they are completed throughout the week.
Parents can detail tasks that should be completed and the monetary amount earned for that task.
As a task is completed it is linked to the child that completed it.
The children should be able to see the available tasks and the tasks they have completed that week.
At the end of each week the children can see the total amount they have earned that week.
The Parents can also see a report of how much each child has earned and the tasks they completed so that they can provide the pocket money earned to each child.
At the start of the next week all tasks become available again to be claimed.
Historical task data shall be available.
The resulting system shall be able to operate on iOS 16, Android 9 and Windows 11 or later.

original_descriptions: Provide children visibility into earning opportunities and personal progress
Develop child financial awareness through regular earnings feedback

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL ZachmanCell elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Task claiming process enabling children to register completed work
payload.justification: Core strategic process allowing children to claim completed tasks for payment throughout the week.

payload.description: Task definition process by which parents set expectations and rewards
payload.justification: Parents defining task expectations and associated monetary rewards represents a strategic configuration process.

payload.description: Opportunity visibility process providing children access to available tasks
payload.justification: Making opportunities visible to children is a strategic process for work distribution and engagement.

payload.description: Weekly earnings feedback process informing children of accumulated pocket money
payload.justification: Providing weekly earnings feedback is a strategic process supporting financial awareness development.

payload.description: Parental oversight reporting process enabling disbursement responsibility fulfillment
payload.justification: Oversight reporting for disbursement responsibility is a strategic control and governance process.

payload.description: Task as a strategically important unit of work eligible for pocket money
payload.justification: Signals explicitly reference tasks as core elements that children claim and parents define with monetary rewards. This is a fundamental business entity at the strategic level.

payload.description: Pocket money as monetary value earned through task completion
payload.justification: Pocket money appears across multiple signals as the core financial entity associated with rewards, disbursement, and earnings tracking.

payload.description: Task opportunity as available work visible to children
payload.justification: Signal identifies opportunities as distinct strategic entities providing visibility into what work is available to claim.

payload.description: Achievement record tracking child task completion history
payload.justification: Personal achievement tracking implies a strategic entity capturing historical completion data for children.

payload.description: Historical record maintained for accountability purposes
payload.justification: Historical record-keeping capability indicates a strategic entity preserving transaction and activity history.

payload.description: Task to pocket money reward association defined by parents
payload.justification: Signal explicitly states parents define the association between tasks and monetary rewards, indicating a strategic relationship.

payload.description: Weekly cycle governing task completion period and earnings accumulation
payload.justification: References to 'throughout the week' and 'weekly earnings feedback' establish a strategic weekly operational cycle.

payload.description: Task completion event triggering pocket money entitlement
payload.justification: Completing a task is the strategic event that creates an earnings claim for children.

payload.description: Child as primary task performer and pocket money earner
payload.justification: Children are consistently identified as the key actors claiming tasks, viewing opportunities, tracking achievement, and receiving earnings feedback.

payload.description: Parent as task definer, reward setter, and disbursement authority
payload.justification: Parents are explicitly described as defining tasks, setting rewards, and fulfilling disbursement responsibilities—key strategic actors.

payload.description: Enable children to earn pocket money through task completion
payload.justification: Core strategic goal explicitly stated as the purpose of enabling children to claim completed tasks for pocket money.

payload.description: Establish clear task expectations and reward structure for children
payload.justification: Parental motivation to define expectations and rewards represents a strategic goal for clarity and structure.

payload.description: Provide children visibility into earning opportunities and personal progress to develop financial awareness
payload.justification: Child need for opportunity visibility and achievement tracking reflects strategic goals for engagement and motivation. | Purpose of providing weekly earnings feedback to support financial awareness is an explicit strategic goal for child development.

payload.description: Fulfill parental oversight and disbursement responsibilities
payload.justification: Parental need for oversight reporting to fulfill disbursement responsibilities represents a strategic goal for accountability.

payload.description: Maintain accountability through historical activity records
payload.justification: Historical record-keeping for accountability and review purposes reflects a strategic goal for transparency and auditability.

payload.name: Family Participation Structure
payload.description: Defines the fundamental roles and relationships within the household economy, establishing who holds authority to create obligations, who performs work, and the nature of their mutual accountability in the earning and reward system.

payload.name: Work-Reward Entitlement
payload.description: Governs the enterprise commitment to compensate completed work with monetary value, including what constitutes rewardable work, how entitlement is triggered, and the cycle within which earning obligations are fulfilled.

payload.name: Opportunity Transparency
payload.description: Ensures that earning possibilities are visible and accessible to those entitled to participate, establishing the enterprise obligation to communicate available work and expected rewards clearly.

payload.name: Performance Recognition
payload.description: Addresses the enterprise responsibility to acknowledge work performed, register claims of completion, and provide participants with awareness of their accumulated entitlements and progress.

payload.name: Parental Oversight Obligation
payload.description: Establishes the authority and responsibility of parents to define expectations, set reward structures, monitor fulfillment, and execute disbursement in accordance with household governance.

payload.name: Historical Accountability
payload.description: Ensures the enterprise maintains enduring records of activity, enabling verification, dispute resolution, and transparency regarding what was performed, earned, and distributed over time.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Domain elements present in the ledger.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a structure in which designated participants perform work activities and receive monetary rewards.
payload.rationale: Expresses the fundamental composition of the household economy: the enterprise comprises participants who perform tasks and earn rewards. This is a structural relationship defining what the enterprise consists of at the scope level.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain governance authority to define obligations and set reward values.
payload.rationale: Establishes the governance rule that the enterprise retains authority over task definition and reward determination. This is a constraint on the distribution of decision-making power within the enterprise.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide a mechanism associating completed work with corresponding monetary rewards.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise commitment to link task completion to pocket money disbursement. This is a functional capability the enterprise must provide to fulfill its accountability in the reward system.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall be accountable for disbursement of earned rewards.
payload.rationale: Captures the enterprise's obligation to execute the payment function once work is completed and rewards are determined. This is distinct from the authority to set rewards.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall recognise completed tasks as the basis for pocket money entitlement.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise commitment linking task completion to monetary reward; integrates the entity definitions (task, pocket money), the triggering event, and the overarching goal into a single normative rule governing entitlement.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a weekly cycle for task completion and earnings accumulation.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise obligation to maintain a temporal rhythm governing work and reward; the weekly cycle is the structural timeframe within which entitlement is realised.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall ensure that task opportunities are visible to children entitled to participate.
payload.rationale: Consolidates the entity (task opportunity) and the process (visibility) into a single enterprise obligation to provide access to available work.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish clear task expectations and reward structure for children.
payload.rationale: Expresses a governance commitment derived from Why-column content to define the framework within which opportunities are communicated.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain records of work performed by participants.
payload.rationale: Achievement record tracking at Row 1 establishes the enterprise's commitment to maintain a record of completion history, without prescribing the system mechanism for storage.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a process enabling participants to register claims of completed work.
payload.rationale: The task claiming process represents an enterprise-level capability that must be provided to participants, expressing the commitment to accept and recognize completion claims.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall provide participants with regular visibility into their accumulated entitlements.
payload.rationale: Weekly earnings feedback process and the goal of providing visibility into earning opportunities converge on a single obligation: the enterprise must inform participants of their progress and accumulated entitlements on a regular basis.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall enable participants to develop financial awareness through transparency of earning opportunities and personal progress.
payload.rationale: The Why-column goal establishes a governance principle that constrains how the enterprise must design its recognition and feedback capabilities: they must promote financial awareness as an enterprise commitment.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall establish a process by which expectations and reward structures are defined.
payload.rationale: Establishes the enterprise-level commitment to govern the definition of expectations and rewards, derived from the task definition process CCI. The How-column process content expresses a governance obligation (how expectations are set) rather than a functional capability.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain oversight reporting to fulfill disbursement responsibilities.
payload.rationale: Expresses the enterprise's obligation to maintain oversight reporting (action) that enables fulfillment of disbursement responsibility. The How-column process content describes a capability the enterprise must provide.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall be accountable for parental oversight and disbursement execution.
payload.rationale: Captures the enterprise-level accountability commitment derived from the Why-column goal. This is a governance constraint expressing what the enterprise is responsible for, not how it achieves it.

payload.statement: The enterprise shall maintain historical records of activity for accountability purposes.
payload.rationale: Both CCIs converge on a single enterprise obligation: the Why-column states the accountability goal (motivation) and the What-column names the historical record entity that serves that goal. This is a governance constraint — the enterprise commits to preserving activity records to enable verification, dispute resolution, and transparency over time.

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Requirement elements present in the ledger.

payload.title: High Level Description
payload.description: Section: High Level Description

payload.completeness_rule: This register SHALL contain the identifiers of ALL Segment elements present in the ledger.

payload.description: Expresses the core purpose of enabling children to claim completed tasks for pocket money throughout the week.
Articulates the parental motivation to define task expectations and associated monetary rewards.
Identifies the child's need for visibility into available opportunities and personal achievement tracking.
Addresses the purpose of providing children weekly earnings feedback to support financial awareness.
Establishes the parental need for oversight reporting to fulfill pocket money disbursement responsibilities.
Indicates a requirement for historical record-keeping capability driven by accountability or review purposes.
